title: Inside Climate News
description: Pulitzer Prize-winning, nonpartisan reporting on the biggest crisis facing our planet.

items.title: The National Park Service Saw Major Job Losses in the Last Year. More Changes Loom.
items.description: Just over a year ago, the Trump administration gutted staff across the National Park Service, triggering a series of protests around the country, a signal of the public’s deep passion for America’s “crown jewels.”  Since then, the service has been in flux. Though a federal judge required the administration to rehire much of the staff […]

items.title: Tiny Texas School District Rejects Tax Deal with $6 Billion LNG Project
items.description: The Point Isabel Independent School District on Monday rejected a multi-million dollar tax break for a proposed $5.7 billion liquefied natural gas (LNG) project on the Texas Gulf Coast, finding the facility would not “align” with the community’s values or finances.  Districts in Texas have typically granted such agreements, which are meant to incentivize investment […]

items.title: New Lawsuit Aims to Halt Expansion of a Montana Coal Mine Blamed for Drying up the Land Above It
items.description: ROUNDUP, Mont.—Environmental groups sued Tuesday to halt an expansion of the Bull Mountains Mine, claiming that the “energy emergency” underpinning its revival is nonexistent and that regulators have been aware for decades that underground coal mining would irrevocably damage the area’s water. The case, filed in the U.S. District Court for the District of Montana, […]

items.title: First Confirmed Cases of Bird Flu in California Elephant Seals Stoke Fear As Virus Surges Worldwide
items.description: Last week, a day that ecologists and virologists in California have feared for years finally arrived.  Officials confirmed seven weaned northern elephant seal pups tested positive for highly pathogenic avian influenza (H5N1) amid a national surge of the deadly virus. When it tore through multiple colonies of the closely related southern elephant seal in South […]

items.title: Scientists, Engineers and Legal Experts Condemn Partisan Attack on Scientific Reference Manual for Judges
items.description: Scientists, engineers, lawyers, statisticians and other experts sounded the alarm Monday about the recent decision to remove a chapter on climate science from an influential reference manual designed to help judges understand complex scientific evidence. For more than 30 years, federal judges have turned to the Reference Manual on Scientific Evidence for guidance on interpreting […]

items.title: Grassroots Resistance to Data Centers Rises in Pennsylvania
items.description: Plans to build more than 50 data centers in Pennsylvania face opposition from a growing network of community groups, environmental activists and state lawmakers from both major parties. Driven by concerns about the industry’s huge electricity demands, heavy water consumption, use of polluting diesel generators for backup power and industrialization of rural areas, an increasing […]

items.title: Environmental Groups Challenge Air Permit for Natural Gas Expansion at Atlanta Plant
items.description: Atlanta has spent decades battling smog and air pollution. Now, state regulators have cleared the way for a major natural gas expansion at Georgia Power’s Plant Bowen, a massive coal-fired power plant roughly 40 miles northwest of downtown that could add hundreds of tons of new air pollution each year to a region already struggling […]

items.title: War in Iran Could Have ‘Historic’ Disruptions on Energy Markets
items.description: The U.S. and Israeli war against Iran is disrupting energy markets and driving oil and gas prices higher in the United States and globally. While those increases are modest so far, experts say the war has the potential to cause more severe and lasting impacts if Iran damages the region’s energy infrastructure or restricts shipping […]

items.title: Dow Asks Texas to Legalize Plastic Pollution From Its Seadrift Complex
items.description: Two weeks ago, when Texas sued a massive Dow petrochemical plant over water pollution, state environmental regulators were already considering a novel proposal from the company that would effectively legalize discharges of plastic material from the 4,700–acre complex into waters feeding San Antonio Bay and the Gulf of Mexico.  If approved by the Texas Commission […]

items.title: Why Electricity Bills Are So High—and How the Blowback Could Hit Trump
items.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio—Protestors stood in the snow outside the offices of Ohio’s utility regulator in January to say they were fed up with rising electricity rates. Even a few years ago, the scene would have been hard to imagine, considering the complexity of utility costs and the obscurity of state regulatory agencies. But rate hikes in […]
